question: Glissant's concept of 'Relation'
options: The dissolution of boundaries — between self and world, past and present
An open, rhizomatic model of culture replacing root-based identity
The Caribbean as a space that mirrors and disrupts the social order
How the physical landscape shapes psychological and cultural identity
answer: An open, rhizomatic model of culture replacing root-based identity

question: The rhizome metaphor (Deleuze and Guattari) applied to Caribbean identity
options: Caribbean writing moves fluidly between standard English and deep creole
Identity grows outward in all directions rather than from a single root
An open, rhizomatic model of culture replacing root-based identity
The complex, mutual transformation of cultures when they meet
answer: Identity grows outward in all directions rather than from a single root

options: The complex, mutual transformation of cultures when they meet
Identity grows outward in all directions rather than from a single root

answer: The complex, mutual transformation of cultures when they meet
